claims.evidence_text: The recommended dose of NPK (120:90:60 kg ha-1 ) were applied as basal dose. Full dose of P and K and half of N dose were applied and mixed with the soil during seed bed preparation. The remaining half of the N-dose was applied with first irrigation.
claims.claim_summary: Recommended N dose of 120 kg/ha for spring wheat; half basal and half with first irrigation.

claims.evidence_text: The recommended dose of NPK (120:90:60 kg ha-1 ) were applied as basal dose.
claims.claim_summary: Recommended P dose of 90 kg/ha (basal) for spring wheat.

claims.evidence_text: The recommended dose of NPK (120:90:60 kg ha-1 ) were applied as basal dose.
claims.claim_summary: Recommended K dose of 60 kg/ha (basal) for spring wheat.

claims.evidence_text: Full dose of P and K and half of N dose were applied and mixed with the soil during seed bed preparation. The remaining half of the N-dose was applied with first irrigation.
claims.claim_summary: Half N applied basal at seedbed preparation, remaining half with first irrigation.

claims.evidence_text: P1 = (Rows 30 cm apart in case of flat planting), P2 = (30 cm apart rows 3rd row skip, in case of flat planting)
claims.claim_summary: Flat-planted wheat sown in rows 30 cm apart.

claims.evidence_text: Line sowing on beds produced significantly more grains spike-1 (53.75) than broadcast on beds (48.31).
claims.claim_summary: Grains per spike ranged from 48.31 (broadcast on beds) to 53.75 (line sowing on beds) in spring wheat.

claims.evidence_text: Planting methods bed planting 3-rows and flat planting 3rd row skip produced heavier grains 45.41 and 44.06 g respectively when compared with other planting methods.
claims.claim_summary: Thousand grain weight 44.06-45.41 g for the best planting methods (bed planting 3-rows and flat 3rd-row skip).

claims.evidence_text: planting method flat sowing in rows produced the highest (444.19) numbers of productive tillers followed by bed sowing 3-rows (397.66) while the lowest (291.53) productive tillers m-2 was produced by bed sowing broadcast.
claims.claim_summary: Productive tillers ranged 291.53-444.19 per m2 across planting methods in spring wheat.
